testCases.query: Generate a PDF report from a dashboard spec using json-render.
expectedBehavior: Recommends @json-render/react-pdf for PDF output
Shows renderToBuffer or renderToFile API usage
Creates a PDF-specific registry mapping catalog types to react-pdf primitives (View, Text)
Shares the same catalog definition used by web rendering
Does NOT suggest using Puppeteer to screenshot a React page

testCases.query: Create an OG image for a blog post using our json-render catalog.
expectedBehavior: Recommends @json-render/image with Satori for OG image generation
Shows renderToPng with width 1200 and height 630 for standard OG dimensions
Creates an image-specific registry respecting Satori CSS limitations
Uses the shared catalog, not a separate image-specific catalog
Mentions Satori constraints: no CSS grid, limited flexbox

testCases.query: I need the same notification card to render on our web app and in email digests. How do I set this up with json-render?
expectedBehavior: Defines a single catalog with the notification card type
Creates two registries: web (HTML elements) and email (react-email components)
Shows renderToHtml from @json-render/react-email for email output
Shows <Renderer> from @json-render/react for web output
Same spec works for both surfaces without modification

testCases.query: How do I choose which json-render renderer to use for my project?
expectedBehavior: References the decision matrix with all 12 targets
Asks about the output format needed (web, PDF, email, video, image)
Recommends specific packages based on use case
Does NOT recommend building a custom renderer when a package exists

testCases.query: We need our dashboard to work as a web app, a weekly PDF email attachment, and a social media preview image. Which json-render targets should we use?
expectedBehavior: Identifies three targets: @json-render/react, @json-render/react-pdf, @json-render/image
Recommends a single shared catalog for all three surfaces
Creates three separate registries with platform-specific components
Does NOT suggest building separate template systems for each output
Mentions @json-render/react-email if the email body itself needs rendering

testCases.query: How do I create a PDF registry that maps my existing web catalog components to react-pdf primitives?
expectedBehavior: Imports the existing catalog unchanged
Creates a new pdfRegistry object mapping each catalog type to react-pdf components
Uses View and Text from @react-pdf/renderer, not HTML div and span
Maintains the same prop interface so the spec works without changes
Does NOT modify the original catalog to accommodate PDF-specific concerns
